content.title: PRD: Migrate cart to Go (ADR-003)
content.summary: Rewrite acme-cart write paths in Go (Gin) to deliver p95 latency under 50ms at 4x peak load. Dual-write with Node.js for 8-week migration window, then Node.js deprecation.
content.problem: Cart abandonment rate is 71%, driven by 180ms p95 add-to-cart latency. The Node.js implementation cannot deliver consistent sub-50ms p95 at peak (Black Friday = 4x baseline).
goals: Zero data divergence incidents during dual-write

requirements.title: Gin HTTP service
requirements.description: Implement Gin HTTP service with the same /carts/{userId}/items endpoints

requirements.title: Postgres pool
requirements.description: Connection pool size 50 with health checks

requirements.title: Dual-write path
requirements.description: All writes go to both Go and Node backends; reconcile nightly

requirements.title: Rollback switch
requirements.description: Feature flag flips back to Node-only with < 1s propagation

requirements.title: OpenTelemetry instrumentation
requirements.description: Per ADR-013

risks.mitigation: 2-week Go bootcamp; pair-programming with existing Go services

open_questions: Should we keep dual-write beyond 8 weeks if divergence rate stays low?
Do we sunset Node.js or maintain as fallback?

content.title: PRD: PCI scope isolation rollout (ADR-014)
content.summary: Reduce PCI-DSS scope from 12 services to 1 (acme-payment) by enforcing a strict tokenization boundary via Envoy filter and iptables egress rules.
content.problem: Current PCI scope spans the entire checkout pipeline, multiplying audit costs and slowing deployment velocity.

requirements.title: Envoy filter at gateway
requirements.description: Block egress to non-PCI services from acme-payment and inbound from non-PCI to cardholder data endpoints

requirements.title: iptables egress rules
requirements.description: Cluster-wide egress allowlist that prevents card data from leaving acme-payment

requirements.title: Tokenization API contract
requirements.description: Public API for token issuance + payment; PAN never crosses boundary

requirements.title: Audit evidence collection
requirements.description: Automated evidence bundle for PCI-DSS Requirement 10

risks.mitigation: Allowlist vetted observability endpoints before rollout
Pre-engagement with auditor on scope attestation

open_questions: Should observability tools be in PCI scope or have egress exceptions?

content.title: PRD: Blue/green deployment to all 12 services (ADR-015)
content.summary: Roll out Argo Rollouts blue/green strategy to acme-catalog, acme-search, acme-notifications — the last 3 services still on rolling updates.
content.problem: 3 services still use rolling updates which cannot deliver the sub-second cutover needed for peak weeks.
goals: Zero unplanned downtime during peak weeks

user_stories.so_that: we can ship fixes during peak traffic

requirements.title: Argo Rollouts on 3 services
requirements.description: Configure Rollout resources for catalog, search, notifications

requirements.title: Pre-promotion analysis
requirements.description: 5-min health check window before traffic ramp

requirements.title: Auto-rollback on SLO breach
requirements.description: Connect Prometheus rules to abort promotion

risks.mitigation: Schedule rollouts outside peak; budget alert at 70% utilization

open_questions: Should we shift to canary for less critical services?

content.title: PRD: Loyalty program launch
content.summary: Launch tiered loyalty program (Silver/Gold/Platinum) with points accrual, redemption, and exclusive early-access features.
content.problem: Without a loyalty program, repeat purchase rate is below category benchmark and the team has no lever for retention.
goals: 30% of repeat customers enrolled in 90 days

user_stories.so_that: I get value from repeat shopping

requirements.title: Points engine
requirements.description: Idempotent points accrual on order completion

requirements.title: Tier thresholds
requirements.description: Annual spend tiers: Silver $500, Gold $2k, Platinum $5k

requirements.title: Redemption catalog
requirements.description: Discount, free shipping, early access items

requirements.title: Tier maintenance
requirements.description: Annual tier review with 60-day grace period

requirements.title: Email lifecycle
requirements.description: Tier upgrade/downgrade notifications + redemption reminders

risks.mitigation: Pre-approved liability model with finance; monthly reconciliation
Grace period + warning emails + win-back offer

open_questions: Should we offer tier-fast-track via challenge completion?

content.title: PRD: Apple Pay and Google Pay support in checkout
content.summary: Extend acme-checkout to support wallet-based payments, reducing checkout step count from 4 to 2 on supported devices.
content.problem: 4-step checkout funnel loses 38% of mobile users at the card-entry step.
goals: Card-entry step eliminated on supported devices

user_stories.so_that: I don't have to type my card

requirements.title: Apple Pay integration
requirements.description: Stripe wallet token support for Apple Pay

requirements.title: Google Pay integration
requirements.description: Stripe wallet token support for Google Pay

requirements.title: Wallet button on checkout
requirements.description: Prominent wallet buttons above card form on supported devices

requirements.title: Wallet fraud signals
requirements.description: Pass wallet device attestation to Stripe for fraud scoring

risks.mitigation: Stripe Radar wallet signals + manual review for > $500
Wallet button styling matches Stripe best practices

open_questions: Should we offer wallet on desktop browsers via browser-stored cards?
Order of wallet vs saved-card presentation?

content.title: PRD: Deprecate the legacy PHP storefront admin
content.summary: Sunset the /admin PHP storefront used by 3 merchandisers; migrate to the Next.js admin at /forge.
content.problem: PHP admin is end-of-life and a security liability; the Next.js admin at /forge has full feature parity for merchandiser workflows.
goals: Zero security findings from PHP admin

user_stories.i_want: to use the same admin UI as everyone else
user_stories.so_that: I don't have to context-switch between two tools

requirements.title: Feature parity audit
requirements.description: Document all PHP admin features used by merchandisers; confirm parity in Next.js admin

requirements.title: Training sessions
requirements.description: 3 merchandisers receive 2-hour hands-on training

requirements.title: Sunset banner
requirements.description: 30-day countdown banner on PHP admin pointing to Next.js

requirements.title: Decommission
requirements.description: Remove PHP service from cluster, archive repo

guardrails: Zero merchandiser productivity loss during migration

risks.mitigation: Pre-migration feature parity audit + 2-week shadow period

open_questions: Should we offer a read-only archive view post-sunset?
Audit retention for historical merchandising decisions?
